Recommended citation: Framstad, E., Storaunet, K. O., Snoksrud, O. and Halbritter, A. H. 2026. NO_DWTO (ID(s): NO_DWTO_001) v. 000.001. ecRxiv: https://github.com/NINAnor/ecRxiv/tree/main/indicators/NO_DWTO_001

TipMetadata
TipLog
    1. May 2026 - Original PR

1. Summary

  1. Background and rationale:

  2. Methods: Data for this indicator come from the NFI, where standing and lying dead wood volume per hectare is recorded for each plot. For each reporting period, area-weighted means of dead wood are calculated for sub-counties, regions and nationally. Each sub-county is scaled against its own reference level before regional and national values are calculated as area-weighted means of scaled sub-county values.

  3. Interpretation: An indicator value of 1 corresponds to dead wood volume per hectare at the sub-county reference level X100 (m³ ha⁻¹). Values decrease toward zero as dead wood volume falls below that reference.

  4. Results and development needs: The indicator is ready for use at sub-county resolution and above. It should not be interpreted at finer spatial resolution than the sub-county reference levels allow.

1.2 Native language summary - Norwegian

Bakgrunn og begrunnelse:

Metoder: Data kommer fra Landsskogtakseringen (NFI). Indikatoren er død vedvolum per hektar på hver takstflate. Arealvektede gjennomsnitt beregnes for delfylker, regioner og nasjonalt nivå.

Tolkning: Indikatorverdier skaleres mot delfylkesvis referansenivå X0 = 0 m³ ha⁻¹ og delfylkespesifikk X100 (m³ ha⁻¹).

Resultater og utviklingsbehov: Indikatoren er klar til bruk på delfylkesnivå og oppover.

2. About the underlying data

The volume of standing and lying dead wood > 10 cm in diameter is recorded for each NFI inventory plot (Viken 2021). There are about 12 000 NFI plots in forest ecosystems, distributed on a regular grid, with varying grid density for forest below/above the coniferous forest line and in Finnmark county. The indicator value is an area-weighted mean of dead wood volume per hectare on included plots. Plots with missing dead wood volume are excluded before aggregation. Results are reported for counties or sub-counties, regions and nationally. All NFI plots are sampled over a 5-year period, with a representative sampling scheme every year, allowing unbiased estimates from data for each year. For this indicator data from each full inventory cycle of 5 years are used, starting in 2010, meaning that results reported for 2024 are based on data from the years 2020-2024. The data are available upon request from NFI.

2.1 Spatial and temporal extent, resolution, and representativity

Data are available from 2010, when the volume of lying dead wood was first recorded based on current methods. A different method for quantifying the volume of dead wood was applied during the 1994-1998 forest inventory. Annual estimates are possible due to representative sampling every year. For this indicator all data from each full inventory cycle of 5 years are used, starting in 2010. The data are representative for all productive forest land of Norway. Aggregated reporting uses counties, regions and the whole country.

2.2 Sampling design

2.3 Original units

The indicator is specified as dead wood total volume per hectare (m³ ha⁻¹), comprising standing and lying dead wood. Aggregated values are area-weighted means of plot-level volume per hectare.

2.4 Instructions for citing, using and accessing data

The data used for this indicator have been made available by the Norwegian National Forest Inventory under a specific data use agreement of 2026-05-29, between NIBIO and the specified users. Further use of these data requires a separate data user agreement with NIBIO.

3. Indicator properties

3.1 Ecosystem Condition Typology Class (ECT)

B1 Biotic compositional characteristic

3.2 Ecosystem condition characteristic

3.3 Collinearities with other indicators

3.4 Impact factors

3.5 Spatial aggregation

The spatial aggregation pathway is Aggregate (NFI plots to sub-county) – [Scale – Truncate] – Aggregate (geographic region) – Aggregate (national level). For each reporting period, non-scaled values for sub-county, region, and Norway are obtained by pooling all included forest plots in the unit: the area-weighted mean of plot-level dw_prha. Sub-county codes are assigned from NFI county labels before aggregation. Scaling and truncation are applied after non-scaled sub-county aggregates have been calculated: each sub-county is scaled against its own lower reference level X0 (0 m³ ha⁻¹) and upper reference level X100 (m³ ha⁻¹) using a linear scaling function, and values above 1 are truncated to 1 at that step. Regional and national scaled point estimates are area-weighted means of the already scaled sub-county values, using each sub-county’s represented area as weight. Non-scaled regional and national point estimates are calculated directly from all plots within each region or the whole country.

4. Reference condition and levels

4.1 Reference condition

The reference condition is considered to be intact forest ecosystems where effects of human activities are clearly subordinate to those of natural ecosystem processes and disturbances.

4.2 Reference levels

Reference values are expert assessments without estimated uncertainty. The lower reference level X0 is set to 0 m³ ha⁻¹ and the upper reference level X100 is set separately for each sub-county. The sub-county X100 values used in the analyses are listed in Section 9.4. For lack of better knowledge, the relationship between indicator values and ecosystem condition is assumed to be linear for values between X0 and X100. Hence, indicator values are scaled between 0 and 1 based on a linear scaling function between X0 and X100; scaled indicator values above 1 are truncated to 1.

4.2.1 Spatial resolution and validity

Reference levels are defined at county or sub-county resolution, not as one national value. Scaling is therefore applied to area-weighted non-scaled indicator values that have first been aggregated to the matching county or sub-county unit. Regional and national reporting uses area-weighted aggregation of these scaled county or sub-county values rather than one common X100 for the aggregated non-scaled value. The indicator should not be interpreted at finer spatial resolution than the county or sub-county reference levels allow.

5. Uncertainties

5.1 General uncertainties

Uncertainties relate to the variability of dead wood volume among NFI plots and to the measurements of this volume for each NFI plot. This combined uncertainty is quantified by bootstrapping. There is also uncertainty related to the setting of the upper reference value and to the shape of the relationship between indicator values and ecosystem condition. These uncertainties are not quantified.

5.2 Reported uncertainties

The uncertainties reported in Section 10 are inferential and refer to uncertainty around the point estimates, not to the full spatial variation in dead wood volume among NFI plots. For each reporting period, non-scaled indicator values are bootstrapped by resampling NFI plots with replacement within each county or sub-county and recalculating the area-weighted mean of dw_prha. For scaled county or sub-county values, bootstrap draws are transformed with ecTools::ec_normalise() using the same county or sub-county-specific reference levels as the point estimates (truncation at scaling). For scaled regional and national values, uncertainty is propagated from the scaled sub-county bootstrap draws with ecTools::ec_upscale(). The resulting distributions are summarised with median and quantile intervals.

6. References

Viken, K. O. 2021. Landsskogtakseringens Feltinstruks — 2021. Vol. 7. 5. NIBIO Bok.

7. Import and prepare datasets

7.1 Dead wood volume per hectare

The volume of standing and lying dead wood per hectare is quantified for each NFI plot according to methods described in (Viken 2021). For this indicator data from each full 5-yr inventory cycle, starting in 2010, are used.

Load data 
# Load main data file
# This export wraps each physical line in an extra pair of double quotes, so a
# single read_csv() sees one column spanning the whole file. Read once as one
# character column per line (readr unwraps the outer quotes), then parse the
# inner CSV text.
lsk_inner_csv <- read_csv(
  here("data/LSK_data_okologisk_tilstand_2026.csv"),
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
) |>
  pull(1) |>
  paste(collapse = "\n")

lsk_data_raw <- read_csv(
  I(lsk_inner_csv),
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8")
) |>
  clean_names()

8. Spatial units

Aggregated indicator values are reported for counties or sub-counties, regions and nationally. County or sub-county codes are assigned from NFI county labels before aggregation. Aggregated indicator values should not be applied for spatial units finer than those that allow reliable estimates.

9. Analyses

9.1 Prepare data and create derived variables

This section prepares the NFI plot data for calculating the indicator.

First we define the 5-year reporting periods that match the temporal structure of the indicator.

Define reporting periods 
# Define multiple 5-year reporting periods starting from 2010
reporting_periods <- list(
  "2010-2014" = 2010:2014,
  "2015-2019" = 2015:2019,
  "2020-2024" = 2020:2024
)

The plot-level indicator is dead wood total volume per hectare (dw_prha). We filter the raw dataset to include both productive and unproductive forest plots (arealtype 1 and 12), remove records missing dw_prha, and standardise the geography fields. Area-weighted values are created using au_areal.

Create derived variables 
lsk_data <- lsk_data_raw |>
  filter(
    arealtype %in% c(1, 12)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(dw_prha)) |>
  mutate(
    fylke_name = region_fylke |>
      stringr::str_remove("^\\d+-\\d+\\s+") |>
      stringr::str_remove("^\\d+\\s*") |>
      stringr::str_squish(),
    county_part = assign_bbca_county_part(fylke_name),
    fylke_name_clean = stringi::stri_trans_general(
      stringr::str_squish(
        tolower(
          stringr::str_replace_all(
            normalize_county_parts(fylke_name),
            "[0-9.-]",
            " "
          )
        )
      ),
      "Latin-ASCII"
    ),
    region_code = assign_region_code(fylke_name_clean),
    region_name = assign_region_name(region_code),
    indicator_continuous = dw_prha,
    indicator_continuous_weighted = dw_prha * au_areal
  )

Finally, we retain only the columns required for subsequent aggregation and reporting.

Select downstream fields 
lsk_data <- lsk_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    sesong,
    au_areal,
    arealtype,
    region_code,
    region_name,
    county_part,
    fylke_name,
    fylke_name_clean,
    dw_prha,
    indicator_continuous,
    indicator_continuous_weighted
  )

9.2 Calculate non-scaled area-weighted indicator values

For each reporting period, we calculate non-scaled indicator values separately for counties or sub-counties, regions, and the whole country. Within each geographic unit, the point estimate is the area-weighted arithmetic mean of all NFI plots in that unit: sum of indicator_continuous_weighted divided by sum of au_areal.

Calculate non-scaled values 
 dwto_results <- purrr::imap(
   reporting_periods,
   ~ calculate_period_indicators(lsk_data, .y, .x)
 ) |>
   purrr::compact()
 
 dwto_results_county_all_periods   <- purrr::map_dfr(dwto_results, "county")
 dwto_results_region_all_periods   <- purrr::map_dfr(dwto_results, "region")
 dwto_results_national_all_periods <- purrr::map_dfr(dwto_results, "national")

9.3 Bootstrap draws per sub-county

For each sub-county and period, NFI plots are resampled with replacement 1000 times. Each replicate computes the area-weighted non-scaled mean of dw_prha, giving a distribution of 1000 draws per sub-county.

Bootstrap draws per sub-county (1000 per sub-county per period) 
county_draws_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(dwto_results, function(period_result, period_name) {
  county_meta <- period_result$county |>
    dplyr::select(fylke_name, county_part, total_area, region_code)

  purrr::imap_dfr(
    split(period_result$data, period_result$data$fylke_name),
    function(county_data, county_name) {
      boot <- bootstrap_indicator(county_data, n_bootstrap = 1000)
      dplyr::tibble(
        period           = period_name,
        fylke_name       = county_name,
        sim_id           = seq_along(boot$draws),
        non_scaled_value = boot$draws
      )
    }
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(county_meta, by = "fylke_name")
})

The table below (Table 2) summarises non-scaled bootstrap draws for all reporting periods: the median area-weighted dead wood volume per hectare per sub-county.

9.4 Scale indicator values and aggregate to regional and national levels

Sub-county reference levels are listed below.

Reference values by sub-county 
dwto_reference_values <- dplyr::tibble(
  county_part = c(
    "Øs", "OA", "HeS", "HeN", "OpS", "OpN", "BuS", "BuV", "Ve",
    "TeS", "TeN", "AA", "VA", "Ro", "Ho", "SF", "MR", "ST", "NT",
    "NoS", "NoN", "Tr", "Fi"
  ),
  x100_m3_ha = c(
    60, 60, 60, 50, 60, 50, 60, 50, 60,
    60, 50, 50, 50, 50, 50, 50, 50, 42.5, 42.5,
    42.5, 28, 28, 23
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(x100 = x100_m3_ha)

x0 <- 0
county_reference <- dwto_reference_values |>
  dplyr::select(county_part, x100)

knitr::kable(
  dwto_reference_values |>
    dplyr::select(`Sub-county` = county_part, `X100 (m³ ha⁻¹)` = x100_m3_ha)
)
Sub-county X100 (m³ ha⁻¹)
Øs 60.0
OA 60.0
HeS 60.0
HeN 50.0
OpS 60.0
OpN 50.0
BuS 60.0
BuV 50.0
Ve 60.0
TeS 60.0
TeN 50.0
AA 50.0
VA 50.0
Ro 50.0
Ho 50.0
SF 50.0
MR 50.0
ST 42.5
NT 42.5
NoS 42.5
NoN 28.0
Tr 28.0
Fi 23.0

Each sub-county’s 1000 non-scaled draws from section 9.3 are scaled with ecTools::ec_normalise() (linear, X0 = 0, sub-county-specific X100; truncation at scaling). Figure 1 shows the mapping for each sub-county.

Scale sub-county draws; derive uncertainty and feed ec_upscale 
county_draws_scaled <- county_draws_long |>
  dplyr::inner_join(county_reference, by = "county_part") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    scaled_value = ec_normalise(
      non_scaled_value,
      x0 = x0,
      x100 = x100,
      fun = "linear"
    ),
    nation = "National"
  )

county_scaled_with_uncertainty <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, fylke_name, county_part, total_area, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(scaled_value),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.025)),
    p_25   = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.25)),
    p_75   = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.75)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    dwto_results_county_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, fylke_name, n_plots),
    by = c("period", "fylke_name")
  )
Normalisation diagnostic figure 
norm_plot_data <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::group_by(county_part) |>
  dplyr::slice_sample(n = 100) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

dwto_norm_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  norm_plot_data,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = non_scaled_value,
    y = scaled_value,
    fill = scaled_value
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    stroke = NA, size = 3, alpha = 0.7, shape = 21
  ) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ county_part, scales = "free_x") +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient(low = "red", high = "green") +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) +
  ggplot2::guides(fill = "none") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Dead wood volume (m³ ha⁻¹)",
    y = "Scaled indicator value"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9))

suppressMessages(
  ggsave(
    filename = here::here("img/dwto_norm_plot.png"),
    plot = dwto_norm_plot,
    scale = 1.5
  )
)

dwto_norm_plot

The scaled draws are then passed to ecTools::ec_upscale(), which propagates uncertainty through the spatial hierarchy. For each of 1000 Monte Carlo iterations, one draw is sampled per sub-county and the area-weighted mean gives one regional value; a second ec_upscale() call repeats this from regions to national level.

ec_upscale: sub-county to region (compute once, save RDS) 
dwto_mc_cache_deps <- c(
  here::here("data/LSK_data_okologisk_tilstand_2026.csv"),
  here::here("R/functions.R"),
  here::here("R/NO_DWTO_001.qmd")
)
dwto_mc_rds_regional <- here::here("data/dwto_mc_regional.rds")

if (
  file.exists(dwto_mc_rds_regional) &&
  file.mtime(dwto_mc_rds_regional) >= max(file.mtime(dwto_mc_cache_deps), na.rm = TRUE)
) {
  dwto_mc_regional <- readRDS(dwto_mc_rds_regional)
} else {
  dwto_mc_regional <- ecTools::ec_upscale(
    data        = county_draws_scaled,
    variable    = scaled_value,
    weight      = total_area,
    start_units = fylke_name,
    end_units   = region_code,
    year        = period,
    end_units_name = "region_code",
    n = 1000
  )
  saveRDS(dwto_mc_regional, dwto_mc_rds_regional)
}
ec_upscale: region to national (compute once, save RDS) 
region_area_weights <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::distinct(period, fylke_name, total_area, region_code) |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(region_area = sum(total_area), .groups = "drop")

dwto_mc_regional_for_national <- dwto_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::left_join(region_area_weights, by = c("period", "region_code")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(nation = "National")

dwto_mc_rds_national <- here::here("data/dwto_mc_national.rds")

if (
  file.exists(dwto_mc_rds_national) &&
  file.mtime(dwto_mc_rds_national) >= max(
    c(file.mtime(dwto_mc_cache_deps), file.mtime(dwto_mc_rds_regional)),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
) {
  dwto_mc_national <- readRDS(dwto_mc_rds_national)
} else {
  dwto_mc_national <- ecTools::ec_upscale(
    data        = dwto_mc_regional_for_national,
    variable    = sampled_mean,
    weight      = region_area,
    start_units = region_code,
    end_units   = nation,
    year        = period,
    end_units_name = "nation",
    n = 1000
  )
  saveRDS(dwto_mc_national, dwto_mc_rds_national)
}
Combine regional and national MC draws; build summary tables 
dwto_mc_combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  dwto_mc_regional |> dplyr::select(period, region = region_code, sampled_mean),
  dwto_mc_national |> dplyr::select(period, region = nation, sampled_mean)
)

county_scaled_all_periods <- dwto_results_county_all_periods |>
  dplyr::left_join(county_reference, by = "county_part") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(x100)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    scaled_value = ec_normalise(indicator_value, x0 = x0, x100 = x100, fun = "linear")
  )

region_scaled_agg_all_periods <- dwto_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(scaled_value = median(sampled_mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = assign_region_name(region_code))

national_scaled_agg_all_periods <- dwto_mc_national |>
  dplyr::group_by(period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(scaled_value = median(sampled_mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(region = "National")

region_scaled_agg_with_uncertainty <- dwto_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_25   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.25)),
    p_75   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.75)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = assign_region_name(region_code)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    dwto_results_region_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, region_code, total_area, n_plots),
    by = c("period", "region_code")
  )

national_scaled_agg_with_uncertainty <- dwto_mc_national |>
  dplyr::group_by(period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_25   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.25)),
    p_75   = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.75)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region = "National") |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    dwto_results_national_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, total_area, n_plots),
    by = "period"
  )

truncation_summary <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  dwto_results_county_all_periods |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      dwto_reference_values |> dplyr::select(county_part, reference_value_m3_ha = x100_m3_ha),
      by = "county_part"
    ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(county_reference, by = "county_part") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level = "Sub-county",
      exceeds_threshold = indicator_value > x100,
      original_value_m3_ha = round(indicator_value, 2),
      region_code = NA_character_,
      region_name = NA_character_
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      period, level, region_code, region_name, fylke_name, county_part,
      reference_value_m3_ha, exceeds_threshold, original_value_m3_ha
    ),
  region_scaled_agg_all_periods |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      dwto_results_region_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, region_code, indicator_value),
      by = c("period", "region_code")
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level = "Region",
      exceeds_threshold = scaled_value >= 1,
      original_value_m3_ha = round(indicator_value, 2),
      reference_value_m3_ha = NA_real_,
      fylke_name = NA_character_,
      county_part = NA_character_
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      period, level, region_code, region_name, fylke_name, county_part,
      reference_value_m3_ha, exceeds_threshold, original_value_m3_ha
    ),
  national_scaled_agg_all_periods |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      dwto_results_national_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, indicator_value),
      by = "period"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level = "National",
      exceeds_threshold = scaled_value >= 1,
      original_value_m3_ha = round(indicator_value, 2),
      reference_value_m3_ha = NA_real_,
      region_code = NA_character_,
      region_name = "National",
      fylke_name = NA_character_,
      county_part = NA_character_
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      period, level, region_code, region_name, fylke_name, county_part,
      reference_value_m3_ha, exceeds_threshold, original_value_m3_ha
    )
)

truncation_counts <- truncation_summary |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, level) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_total = dplyr::n(),
    n_exceeding_reference = sum(exceeds_threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_exceeding_reference = round(100 * n_exceeding_reference / n_total, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

truncated_values <- truncation_summary |>
  dplyr::filter(exceeds_threshold) |>
  dplyr::arrange(period, level, dplyr::desc(original_value_m3_ha))

At sub-county level, truncation is evaluated by comparing the non-scaled point estimate to the matching X100. At regional and national level, the tables report cases where the ec_upscale() median equals 1. The following tables summarise how often truncation occurred in each period and list the affected units.

Period Level Total Exceeding reference % Exceeding reference
2010-2014 National 1 0 0.0
2010-2014 Region 5 0 0.0
2010-2014 Sub-county 23 0 0.0
2015-2019 National 1 0 0.0
2015-2019 Region 5 0 0.0
2015-2019 Sub-county 23 0 0.0
2020-2024 National 1 0 0.0
2020-2024 Region 5 0 0.0
2020-2024 Sub-county 23 0 0.0 

No values exceeded their sub-county reference and required truncation.

The time series below (Figure 2) summarises regional and national values.

Time series of indicator values by region and period 
dwto_region_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Øst", "2" = "Sør", "3" = "Vest",
  "4" = "Midt", "5" = "Nord", "National" = "National"
)
dwto_region_levels <- c("National", "Midt", "Nord", "Øst", "Sør", "Vest")
dwto_region_cols <- setNames(
  MetBrewer::met.brewer("Archambault", n = length(dwto_region_levels)),
  dwto_region_levels
)

dwto_plot_data <- dwto_mc_combined |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    region_name = dplyr::recode(region, !!!dwto_region_labels),
    series_type = dplyr::if_else(region == "National", "National", "Region")
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_name, series_type) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = factor(region_name, levels = dwto_region_levels))

ggplot2::ggplot(
  dwto_plot_data,
  ggplot2::aes(x = period, y = median, colour = region_name, group = region_name)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(
    ggplot2::aes(linewidth = series_type),
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.18)
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_pointrange(
    ggplot2::aes(ymin = p_025, ymax = p_975, shape = series_type),
    size = 0.8, linewidth = 0.45,
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.18)
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = dwto_region_cols, name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c(National = 19, Region = 17), name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(National = 1, Region = 0.55), guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "horizontal",
    panel.grid.minor   = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  ggplot2::guides(
    colour = ggplot2::guide_legend(
      nrow = 2,
      override.aes = list(size = 0.7, linewidth = 0.45)
    ),
    shape  = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 0.8, linewidth = 0.45))
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(y = "Indicator value", x = "")

10. Results

The following tables present scaled indicator results at sub-county, regional, and national scales for each reporting period. Tables report the median as the indicator value, with an approximate 95% interval (2.5% and 97.5% quantiles) and an approximate interquartile range (25% and 75% quantiles). All scaled values are on a 0–1 scale, where 0 corresponds to X0 (0 m³ ha⁻¹) and 1 corresponds to the sub-county-specific X100 for sub-county units.

10.1 Sub-county results (scaled with uncertainty)

Use the search box and page controls in Table 3 to browse sub-county rows across all reporting periods.

10.2 Regional results (scaled with uncertainty)

10.3 National results (scaled with uncertainty)

11. Export file

Regional and national Monte Carlo draws are exported in one table (dwto_mc_combined: period, region, sampled_mean; region contains the five NI region codes and "National").

Export regional and national MC-propagated indicator draws 
write.csv(
  dwto_mc_combined,
  here::here("data/results_NO_DWTO_001.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

Study design The study was designed by [AB, CD].

Statistical analysis All analyses were conducted by [CD] using [software], with verification by [AB].

Visualization Figures were produced by [KL], with input from [CD].

Reproducibility All code was reviewed and validated by [AB, CD].

TipSession Info

This information is mainly aimed at developers that need to recreate the R environment that produced the html. Important note: this information is about the R session that was running when the html was rendered, and if some of the code chunks were not evaluated when the html was rendered, they may have been executed in a different environment.
